description: PS5 debugger payload - userland TCP wire-protocol server hosted inside SceShellCore (successor to GoldHEN/ps5debug)

changelog: 🐛 Fixed a **critical bug** - console crash (KP) when rescanning with "between".
No other changes from v1.2.6 revision1 (which added the FW 8.40+ poke/breakpoint fix).
v1.2.5 added *initial* support for FW 8.x-13.x. **v1.2.6 makes it actually functional** and adds hardware verification across most firmwares.
✅ **Hardware-verified: FW 4.x, 6.x, 8.x, 9.x, 10.x and 12.x** - poke, software + hardware breakpoints, all watchpoint types, title ID, content ID and version confirmed working.
⚠️ **FW 3.x, 5.x, 7.x, 11.x and 13.x: supported but not yet fully tested.** The fixes are firmware-agnostic by design. Feedback welcome.

Pokes silently failed on FW ≥ 9 (the kernel blocks the old mdbg write path there). Writes now go through a DMAP page-table walk, so **poking, cheats and patches work again on 9.x-13.x**. (mdbg stays the path on FW ≤ 8.x.)
Newly allocated memory is pre-faulted so writes land correctly.
Hardware breakpoints/watchpoints were silently doing nothing on 9.x+ - now enabled across 9.00-13.20.
Software (INT3) breakpoints now write correctly on 9.x+ (same DMAP fix as poke).
Fixed breakpoints/watchpoints that **fired a few times then stopped** on FW 11/12 (the debug-status register wasn't being cleared between hits).

Game **title ID, content ID and version** are detected by scanning the process instead of per-firmware offsets (which shifted at 8.x and 12.x). Reaper / Cheater again show the running game correctly on newer firmware.
Closing the client mid-operation (e.g. during a scan) could **crash the whole console** by killing SceShellCore. Now suppressed - disconnecting is safe. Affects all firmwares.

**Added initial support for FW 8.x, 9.x, 10.x, 11.x, 12.x and 13.x.** ⚠️ These are *initial support - testing required* (not yet hardware-validated). Feedback from users on these firmwares is welcome.
**FW 6.x and 7.61 are hardware-validated.**
Previously these firmwares failed to install (`SceShellCore not found`) and, even when forced, showed **blank process names** and **truncated memory maps**. All fixed:
Install now succeeds.
`PROC_INFO` / `PROC_LIST` show correct process names.
`PROC_MAPS` returns the complete memory map.
**Firmware-agnostic process lookup** - the installer now locates the target via a stable system call instead of per-firmware kernel offsets, making install more robust across firmwares.
**Kernel-patch guard** - the installer now detects when the jailbreak has *already* applied the kernel patch and skips re-writing it, **preventing a kernel panic** seen on 7.61.
**Corrected per-firmware process-info offsets** - process name / path / title ID / content ID now read correctly on 6.x and newer (were frozen at 4.x values and came back empty on 6.x+).
Added **EchoStretch** and **Sonic-Iso** to the special thanks.
Features
CMD_PROC_MAPS now augments the vm_map list with Sony-aux regions
(SceGnm/SceAgc/JIT/DMEM) that bypass vm_map, discovered via a kernel-side
x86-64 page-table walk over the DMAP. FW-agnostic (runtime pmap-offset
discovery + ground-truth validation); falls back to the plain vm_map result
on any failure.
Hybrid memory-read path: reads route DMAP-direct for vm_map-absent (aux)
regions and mdbg otherwise (and as fallback), improve aux scans performance on
both the CMD_PROC_READ and server-side scan paths. No protocol change;
byte-identical results.
Hotfix: Fixed a rare save-corruption issue
Initial stable public release
